prerequisites: A Paystack account. Use Test Mode keys to read data before your account is activated, or activate the account to use Live Mode keys for production data.
Your Paystack secret key, found in the Dashboard under Settings then the API Keys & Webhooks tab. Only admins and the primary account owner can view or regenerate the secret key.
A start_date value marking the earliest record to sync (required).
Optionally, a lookback_window_days value to re-read records updated within a trailing window on each sync (optional).

authMethods.name: Secret key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Paystack authenticates every request via the HTTP Authorization header using a Bearer token: 'Authorization: Bearer SECRET_KEY'. Supply your account secret key (test keys are prefixed sk_test_, live keys sk_live_) in the connector's secret_key field. Requests without a valid key return 401 Unauthorized, and all requests must use HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Locate your secret key in the Paystack Dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Paystack Dashboard, open the Settings page, and click the API Keys & Webhooks tab. Copy the secret key. Test Mode keys (sk_test_...) work before account activation; Live Mode keys (sk_live_...) require an activated account. If needed, use 'Generate new secret key' to rotate, which requires an admin or primary owner and your password.

setupSteps.title: Provide the secret key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied secret key into the connector's required secret_key field. Keep it confidential and never expose it in client-side code or version control. The connector sends it as 'Authorization: Bearer <secret_key>' on every API call.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date to the earliest point from which records should be synced. Optionally set lookback_window_days to re-fetch records updated within that trailing number of days on each incremental run; leave it unset to use the connector default.

sources.title: Paystack Support: API keys and webhooks
